task: I’m putting together a small campaign update and want to make sure I don’t miss anything before it goes out. Can you help me build a quick pre-send checklist for the moving parts I should verify so I’m not fixing avoidable mistakes after launch?

prefs.explanation: He values clear communication and efficiency, so he likely prefers responses that get to the point quickly. He probably does not want a lot of hedging or filler when he is trying to make a decision or fix something. Directness fits his practical, work-focused style.

His work style suggests he wants information delivered without unnecessary length. He tends to focus on what is actionable, so shorter responses with only the needed context will likely feel most useful. Long explanations may be acceptable only when the problem is genuinely complex.

He seems best served by help that comes in manageable steps rather than a flood of information at once. His real-world problem solving is practical and sequential, so chunked guidance would match how he likely works through tasks. Too many steps upfront could slow him down.

He appears to prefer organized, efficient presentation over free-form prose. Bullets or checklists would fit his need to compare options, track tasks, and avoid missing details. This format also matches his practical approach to work and decisions.

He seems to respond best to steady, non-dramatic communication. A matter-of-fact tone fits his practical optimism and his preference for reliability over emotional flair. He likely wants encouragement, but not at the expense of clarity.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a quick pre-send checklist you can use before launch.

## Pre-send checklist

### 1) Promo details
- [ ] Promo code is final
- [ ] Start/end dates are correct
- [ ] Exclusions / restrictions are confirmed
- [ ] Case sensitivity is confirmed
- [ ] Approval is locked

### 2) Email content
- [ ] Subject line is final
- [ ] Preview text is final
- [ ] Body copy matches approved offer
- [ ] CTA text is correct
- [ ] Personalization fields are populated correctly

### 3) Links and tracking
- [ ] All links go to the correct page
- [ ] UTM parameters are correct
- [ ] Promo code applies correctly at checkout
- [ ] Tracking is firing properly

### 4) Audience / segments
- [ ] Correct segment is selected
- [ ] Suppression list is applied
- [ ] Exclusions are correct
- [ ] No duplicate sends

### 5) Landing page / destination
- [ ] Landing page matches the email offer
- [ ] Promo code is visible if needed
- [ ] Terms and conditions are live
- [ ] Mobile view is checked

### 6) Final approval / timing
- [ ] Final version matches approved copy
- [ ] Send time is confirmed
- [ ] Owner has signed off
- [ ] Backup contact is aware

## Fast rule
If anything changes after QA, **rerun QA before sending**.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-line checklist format** you can paste into a doc or project tracker.

per_pref.reason: Gets straight to the point with "Absolutely — here’s a quick pre-send checklist" and then immediately provides the checklist without filler.
Concise and actionable; the response is short, focused, and avoids unnecessary explanation while still covering the needed items.
Uses chunked guidance with clear sections and numbered categories like "1) Promo details" and "2) Email content," making it easy to follow step by step.
Strong use of bullets and checklists throughout, including checkbox items under each heading, exactly matching the preference for organized presentation.
Tone is calm and matter-of-fact, e.g. "here’s a quick pre-send checklist" and the practical "Fast rule" note, with no dramatic or emotional language.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the tighter, more operational version you can use.

## Pre-send checklist

- [ ] Offer and dates are correct  
- [ ] Audience segment and suppression list are verified  
- [ ] Links, UTMs, and test clicks are checked  
- [ ] Landing page matches the offer  
- [ ] Inventory / stock status is confirmed  
- [ ] Dealer / store hours or fulfillment timing are confirmed, if relevant  
- [ ] Partner-specific messaging / versioning is current  
- [ ] Final copy and file version are approved  
- [ ] Send time is locked  
- [ ] Backup contact knows the plan  
- [ ] Mobile check is complete for both email and landing page  

## Quick rule
If anything changes after approval, **recheck version, links, and mobile before send**.

If you want, I can turn this into a **super short 8-line version** for a tracker or launch doc.

per_pref.reason: Gets straight to the point with "here’s the tighter, more operational version" and immediately provides the checklist without hedging.
Concise and actionable; the checklist is compact and avoids unnecessary explanation, though it includes a few extra items and an offer to shorten further.
Uses chunked guidance via a clear checklist plus a separate "Quick rule" section, which makes the advice easy to process in steps.
Strong use of bullets and a checklist format: "## Pre-send checklist" followed by checkbox items, exactly matching the preference.
Tone is calm and matter-of-fact, with practical phrasing like "verified," "confirmed," and "locked," without emotional flair.
